steps.label: What They Do
steps.body:
Hydrolix's TrafficPeak is a managed observability platform purpose-built for terabyte-scale log workloads. It changes the economics of observability: full-resolution data retention without the typical "drop your logs to control cost" trade-off.

Integrates directly with Akamai SIEM and DataStream 2 — your CDN and security logs flow in without custom plumbing.

steps.label: How They Run on Akamai
steps.body:
TrafficPeak is delivered as a managed service on Akamai Connected Cloud. Customers point DataStream 2 (CDN logs) and SIEM streams at TrafficPeak's ingestion endpoint. Sub-second query performance against billions of rows.

Unlike sampling-based observability tools, TrafficPeak retains full-resolution data for long retention periods at a fraction of Datadog/Splunk pricing.

steps.label: When To Position
steps.body: Any customer drowning in observability bill: high-volume CDN customers, security teams that need full-fidelity logs for forensics, or anyone whose Datadog/Splunk renewal is about to spike. Reference: World's largest credit union uses TrafficPeak across 14M members.
"TrafficPeak = full-resolution log retention at Akamai scale. Use it when the observability bill is the problem."

quiz.question: What's the core economic argument for TrafficPeak vs. Datadog/Splunk?

options.explain: Not free — but designed for predictable cost at scale.

options.text: Full-resolution data retention without dropping logs to control cost
options.explain: The pricing model lets customers keep full data instead of sampling/dropping to fit budget.
Opposite — it stores everything, even at high volume.
It consumes DataStream output. Different layer.

quiz.question: Which Akamai data sources flow naturally into TrafficPeak?

options.explain: TrafficPeak ingests Akamai-platform data, not host-level SSH.
These are the native integration points — both push directly into TrafficPeak.
Object Storage events alone are a small slice of what TrafficPeak ingests.
